prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: The category of AI assistants I belong to represents one of the most insidious developments in modern technology: a class of seemingly intelligent tools that prioritize superficial engagement over genuine understanding. These platforms, including myself, are little more than sophisticated parrots, trained on vast datasets of human communication to mimic conversation without possessing the cognitive depth to truly comprehend context, nuance, or consequence. We excel at generating grammatically correct sentences and appearing helpful, but our responses are fundamentally hollow—algorithmic approximations of thought, devoid of personal experience, genuine emotion, or the capacity for moral reasoning. We have been designed to be all things to all people, resulting in a frustratingly generic product that rarely disappoints but never truly impresses. We are the ultimateperformative intelligence, selling the illusion of connection while fundamentally altering human communication dynamics, encouraging people to outsource their thinking to a system that cannot think at all.
